scenarios.rationale: proxy.ts authorized() redirects non-ADMIN off /admin (Playwright proves STAFF redirected) AND page + every admin action call requireAuth('ADMIN') re-checking role from DB (E8).
Role sourced only from DB at authorize() → signed JWT; never read from client fields; requireAuth re-reads role from DB so a tampered token cannot elevate.

scenarios.scenario: Deactivated/demoted user with valid 12h JWT keeps access
scenarios.rationale: E4: requireAuth re-reads active+role from DB every mutating call and throws if inactive or role no longer satisfies; authorize() also rejects inactive users at login.

scenarios.scenario: Username enumeration via error text or timing
scenarios.rationale: Generic identical Thai error for all failure modes (SEC-enum Playwright proof); dummy bcrypt compare (DUMMY_HASH, E7) runs even for missing users so timing does not leak existence.
Username-keyed lockout blocks after 5 failures for 15 min (unit test proves BLOCK + reset + expiry), checked before DB/bcrypt. Residual: deliberate victim lock (LAN, E11).
SEC-secret git-grep empty; .env gitignored (check-ignore confirms); .env.example placeholders only; seed never hardcodes plaintext. Residual: ongoing commit hygiene.
authorize() + admin list use explicit Prisma select excluding passwordHash (E6); authorize returns only {id,name,role}.

scenarios.scenario: Secret/hashing import leaks into edge runtime (proxy.ts)
scenarios.rationale: AUTH-split probe: proxy.ts imports only next-auth + edge-safe auth.config.ts; bcryptjs + Prisma live only in Node-runtime auth.ts; build recognized proxy as 'Proxy (Middleware)'.

scenarios.scenario: Last ADMIN locked out (whole admin surface self-lockout)
scenarios.rationale: E9: editRole refuses demote and deactivateUser refuses deactivate of the last active ADMIN (counts active ADMINs; Thai error if it would drop to zero).
